data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs had a total net inflow of US$935 million yesterday, continuing a net inflow for 7 consecutive days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 22, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$935 million.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a single-day net inflow of US$877 million. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow has reached US$47.554 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a single-day net inflow of US$48.6615 million. Currently, FBTC's total net inflow has reached US$11.878 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is US$134.295 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Bitcoin's total market value) is 6.09%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$44.315 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETF had a total net inflow of US$111 million yesterday, continuing its net inflow for 5 consecutive days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 22, Eastern Time), the Ethereum spot ETF had a total net inflow of US$111 million.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a single-day net inflow of US$43.7452 million. Currently, ETHE has a total net outflow of US$4.285 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FETH, with a single-day net inflow of US$42.2495 million. Currently, FETH has a total net inflow of US$1.445 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETF is US$9.335 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Ethereum's total market value) is 2.93%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$2.701 billion.

data.title: Swedish health firm jumps 37% on first Bitcoin buy, China EV seller to buy 1K BTC
data.description:
Shares in Swedish health tech company H100 Group AB rose 37% after it said it purchased Bitcoin for the first time as part of a new strategy, while China’s Jiuzi Holdings revealed its plan to stack 1,000 Bitcoin over the next year. H100 said on May 22 that it spent 5 million Norwegian krone ($490,830) buying 4. 39 Bitcoin (BTC) at an average purchasing price of around $111,785. The company’s shares closed May 22 trading up 37% to 1
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/swedish-health-firm-rises-37-percent-bitcoin-buy?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Canadian Tech Firm Matador Plans Additional Fundraising for Bitcoin Investment
data.description:
According to PANews, Canadian technology company Matador, which has incorporated Bitcoin into its corporate treasury, announced plans to raise an additional CAD 4 million through a private placement. The company intends to issue up to 6,451,613 common shares and may opt to increase the offering by up to 15% for overallotment purposes.

Earlier this month, Matador revealed its intention to raise CAD 3 million, with a portion of the net proceeds earmarked for increasing its Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: The crypto market rose for two consecutive days, BTC rose above $111,000, but SUI Ecosystem fell 2.40%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On May 23, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto market sector rose for two consecutive days. Bitcoin (BTC) continued to rise, rising 1.36% again in 24 hours to above $111,000, continuing to hit a new high. Ethereum (ETH) rose 3.94%, approaching the $2,700 mark. In addition, the AI ​​sector rose 7.44%. Within the sector, Render (RENDER) and Worldcoin (WLD) rose 9.64% and 26.78% respectively.
At the same time, MAG7.ssi rose 2.29%, DEFI.ssi rose 5.43%, and MEME.ssi rose 4.90%.
Other bright spots include: Meme sector rose 6.08% in 24 hours, Fartcoin (FARTCOIN), Bonk (BONK), Pepe (PEPE), dogwifhat (WIF) rose 11.71%, 14.59%, 16.09%, 20.51% respectively; Layer2 sector rose 5.84%, Starknet (STRK) rose 12.90%; DeFi sector rose 4.60%, Jupiter (JUP) rose 17.25%; Layer1 sector rose 4.12%, CeFi sector rose 2.71%, Hyperliquid (HYPE) rose 18.97%; PayFi sector rose 2.67%, SafePal (SFP) rose 13.21%.
The crypto sector index, which reflects the historical market conditions of the sector, shows that the ssiAI, ssiLayer2, and ssiGameFi indices rose by 7.67%, 5.57%, and 5.48% respectively in 24 hours.
In addition, due to the hacker attack on Cetus Protocol of SUI ecology, the SUI ecological sector fell by 2.40% in 24 hours, Sui (SUI) fell by 1.02%, Cetus Protocol (CETUS), SuiPad (SUIP), and SUIA fell by 22.16%, 22.08%, and 24.51% respectively.

data.title: BitMart founder Sheldon Xia was invited to attend Trump's VIP dinner
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On the evening of May 22, Eastern Time, BitMart founder Sheldon Xia was invited to attend the VIP dinner hosted by Trump at the National Golf Club, and discussed the future of the crypto industry with many heavyweight guests.
Trump attended and delivered a speech. Sheldon said that Trump mentioned in his speech that "it will bring more to the crypto world and needs more (participation). Let us build the future of the crypto world together."
The dinner also set up an interactive wall with the theme of Trump Meme and distributed TRUMP series souvenirs to the guests.
It is reported that BitMart recently launched a series of Bitcoin Pizza Day activities with the theme of "Make Tokens Tasty Again", which brings users 0 handling fee deposits, trading rewards and other multiple benefits.

The move isn’t just a technical shuffle, it reflects FIFA’s changing view of what fans actually want from NFTs and how it plans.

data.title: U.S. Man Sentenced for Unlicensed Bitcoin Conversion Service
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a Massachusetts man has been sentenced to six years in prison for operating an unlicensed cash-to-Bitcoin conversion service. Trung Nguyen, from Danvers, was sentenced by Boston federal court Judge Richard Stearns, who also ordered him to forfeit $1.5 million. Following his prison term, Nguyen will undergo three years of supervised release. The Boston U.S. Attorney’s Office announced the sentencing on May 22.

Prosecutors revealed that Nguyen ran a business named National Vending from September 2017 to October 2020, which was not registered as a money-transmitting service. He reportedly used techniques learned from an online course to avoid detection by authorities. These methods included disguising his operations as a vending machine company, accepting cash deposits, listing fictional suppliers, and avoiding the use of the term 'Bitcoin.' This deception allowed him to conduct transactions without drawing attention from banks, crypto exchanges, or state authorities.

Nguyen's clientele included scam victims who were misled into converting cash into Bitcoin by overseas con artists, as well as a drug dealer who transferred $250,000 in cash through ten transactions in 2018. The Justice Department stated that Nguyen converted over $1 million into Bitcoin without registering with the Treasury’s Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN), a requirement under federal Anti-Money Laundering regulations. Additionally, he failed to file Suspicious Activity Reports or Currency Transaction Reports for transactions exceeding $10,000.

Nguyen's illegal activities were uncovered through meetings with undercover law enforcement officers, where he accepted cash and provided Bitcoin in exchange, charging a commission of over 5%. The indictment detailed that Nguyen used encrypted messaging apps and other technologies to obscure his Bitcoin transactions. He also structured cash deposits into smaller amounts over consecutive days and at different bank branches to avoid detection.

Charged with operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business and two counts of money laundering, Nguyen pleaded not guilty in June 2023. However, a jury convicted him in November on the charge of operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business and one count of money laundering, while acquitting him of a separate money laundering charge.

data.title: Uniswap Foundation Announces Q1 2025 Financial Summary: Over $148 Million in Assets and Committed New Grants
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Uniswap Foundation released an unaudited first quarter financial summary ending March 31, 2025. The report shows that as of that date, the foundation held $53.4 million in cash and stablecoins, as well as tokens worth $95 million at the closing price at the time (including 15.8 million UNI and 257 ETH), with main assets totaling approximately $148.4 million.
In the first quarter of 2025, the Uniswap Foundation achieved total revenue of $140.3 million, of which $140 million was raised through governance proposals. In terms of expenditure, operating expenses for the quarter were $1.9 million.
In terms of grants, the foundation made new commitments of $12.4 million in the first quarter and has paid out $2.1 million in committed grants. According to the plan, the foundation expects to have $115.1 million for grant commitments and incentives (including $99.8 million in new commitments from 2025 to 2026), and another $33.3 million for operating expenses and employee token rewards until January 2027.

data.title: Farcaster Founder Criticizes Vitalik Buterin's Stance on Local Nodes
data.description: According to Odaily, Farcaster founder Dan Romero recently expressed strong criticism of Vitalik Buterin's views on local node operation. In a post on Wrapcast, Romero commented on Buterin's earlier long-form post, stating, "It's 2025, and users running nodes locally for personal use should get lost. Those among us who hinder the entire ecosystem's development to cater to these local node operators should also get lost." Romero further addressed Buterin, questioning whether he was listening or if the "ivory tower in Singapore" was only open to those operating local nodes. In response, Buterin suggested that Romero consider selling his ETH.
